title: CPR (Infant)

steps.title: Check responsiveness
steps.instruction: Tap the bottom of the foot and shout 'Are you OK?' Check for breathing for 5-10 seconds. If no response and no breathing, act immediately.

steps.title: Call 101 and get an AED
steps.instruction: If alone, shout for help. If someone is there, have them call 101 and find an AED. If alone and no phone, perform 2 minutes of CPR before calling.

steps.title: Chest compressions (30:2)
steps.instruction: Place the infant on a firm, flat surface. Use the two-thumb-encircling hands technique (preferred) or the heel of one hand in the center of the chest. Push hard and fast — 1.5 inches deep, 100-120 per minute.

steps.title: Open the airway
steps.instruction: Tilt the head back to a 'neutral' or 'sniffing' position. Do NOT over-tilt as it can block an infant’s narrow airway.

steps.title: Give 2 rescue breaths
steps.instruction: Cover BOTH the nose and mouth with your mouth. Give 2 gentle breaths (only enough to see the chest rise). Each breath should last 1 second.

steps.title: Continue cycles
steps.instruction: Continue sets of 30 compressions followed by 2 breaths. Minimize interruptions. Change rescuers every 2 minutes to prevent fatigue.

steps.title: AED Use (if available)
steps.instruction: Apply pediatric pads as soon as available (one on chest, one on back if needed for infants). Follow voice prompts. Immediately resume CPR after any shock.

warnings: Do NOT over-tilt the head — keep it in a neutral position
Do NOT blow too hard — give only small, gentle puffs of air
Do NOT use two-finger technique if you can use encircling thumbs or one-hand heel
sources: Magen David Adom Pediatric Life Support
